steps.title: Use a rational remainder regression
steps.content: Every choice has denominator $x-3$, so fit the expression to the form $x+a+\frac{b}{x-3}$. Choose values that avoid $x=3$.

steps.title: Read the quotient and remainder
steps.content:
The regression gives $a=1$ and $b=-2$, so
$$\frac{x^2-2x-5}{x-3}=x+1-\frac{2}{x-3}$$
That matches choice D.

steps.title: Confirm with one division step
steps.content: Long division gives quotient $x+1$ and remainder $-2$: after subtracting $x(x-3)$, the remainder expression is $x-5$; after subtracting $1(x-3)$, the final remainder is $-2$. Choice D is correct.
